skill.name: firebase-development-validate
skill.description: Review Firebase projects for configuration quality, Firestore rules, authentication patterns, testing coverage, and production readiness.
skill.summary: Validate Firebase code against security, architecture, and release readiness checks.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backtick spans and command examples as external command execution. Review found no executable scripts, prompt injection, network exfiltration, or malicious intent; the examples are project validation checks for Firebase code. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because it may lead an agent to run local grep, npm, and build commands in a user project.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Require User Project Context
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are false positives for executable code. Lines 107-120 and 141-143 show example grep, npm test, npm audit, build, and test commands for validating a Firebase project. These are legitimate review steps, but agents should run them only in the user's intended repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected lines are inside Markdown code blocks or checklist text, not executable skill code. The commands are standard local validation commands with no network destination or credential handling beyond normal package audit behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment File Reference Is a Security Checklist Item
low_findings.description: The static sensitive-file finding at line 136 is a false positive for secret access. The text says to confirm .env files are in .gitignore, which is defensive guidance rather than an instruction to read or exfiltrate environment files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 136 only references .env files as part of a security review checklist. No code reads file contents, prints secrets, or transmits environment data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings at lines 3, 103, and 128 are not cryptographic code. They occur in descriptive text, a TypeScript ABOUTME comment example, and an HTTP status code checklist item.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines do not contain hash functions, encryption calls, or security-sensitive algorithm choices. The scanner appears to have matched incidental text patterns.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Firebase Configuration Review
low_findings.description: The static system reconnaissance finding at line 152 is a false positive. The line asks reviewers to confirm Firebase rewrites reference valid functions, which is normal deployment configuration validation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a Firebase hosting checklist, not host enumeration or system discovery. No command gathers machine, network, or account inventory.

content.value_statement: Firebase projects can ship with weak rules, mixed function patterns, or missing emulator coverage. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured review checklist for secure releases.
actual_capabilities: Checks firebase.json for hosting, functions, Firestore, and emulator configuration.
Reviews Firestore rules for helper functions, default deny rules, and client write validation.
Validates Firebase Functions architecture for consistent Express, domain-grouped, or individual patterns.
Reviews authentication flows for API keys, Firebase Auth, user identity, and emulator setup.
Checks test coverage, build readiness, npm audit status, and production configuration.
Produces severity-categorized findings with remediation recommendations.
limitations: It does not deploy Firebase resources or change cloud configuration.
It depends on access to the project files being reviewed.
It cannot verify Firebase Console settings unless the user provides that context.
It gives review guidance and does not replace a full penetration test.

use_cases.title: Pre-release Firebase audit
use_cases.description: Review rules, functions, tests, and build checks before a production release.

use_cases.title: Firestore rules review
use_cases.description: Find overly broad reads, unsafe writes, missing default deny rules, and inconsistent helper usage.

use_cases.title: Architecture consistency check
use_cases.description: Confirm Firebase Functions follow one clear structure and use consistent response and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Firebase review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Firebase project for configuration, rules, authentication, testing, and production readiness. Summarize critical, important, and minor issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing Firebase codebase

prompt_templates.title: Firestore security focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the Firestore rules and related client writes. Check ownership checks, affected key validation, collection group rules, and default deny coverage.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of data access controls

prompt_templates.title: Functions release readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the Firebase Functions architecture. Check exports, middleware, authentication, error handling, response format, tests, build scripts, and deployment risks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate release review for backend changes

prompt_templates.title: Full production gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a production readiness review for this Firebase project. Correlate firebase.json, Firestore rules, functions architecture, emulator tests, npm audit results, and .firebaserc settings. Return prioritized remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Review my Firebase app before launch.
output_examples.output: Critical: Firestore rules are missing a default deny rule. Important: emulator tests do not cover the write workflow. Minor: firebase.json has no UI emulator setting.

output_examples.input: Check whether my functions architecture is consistent.
output_examples.output: The project mostly follows an Express function pattern. Move two standalone handlers into the shared router or document them as separate deployment units.

output_examples.input: Audit my authentication and API key handling.
output_examples.output: API key middleware validates the prefix and active flag. Add user ownership checks before privileged reads and ensure local development uses the auth emulator.

best_practices: Run the review against the exact branch planned for deployment.
Provide firebase.json, Firestore rules, functions source, tests, and .firebaserc together.
Confirm each finding with local tests or emulator runs before release.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for initial Firebase project scaffolding.
Do not treat checklist completion as proof that production data is secure.
Do not ignore Firebase Console settings that are not visible in the repository.

faq.question: Does this skill change my Firebase project?
faq.answer: No. It reviews files and recommends fixes. Any code or configuration changes require separate user approval.

faq.question: Can it find insecure Firestore rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks common rule problems, including broad access, missing default deny rules, and weak write validation.

faq.question: Does it support Cloud Functions reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks architecture style, exports, middleware, authentication, error handling, and test coverage.

faq.question: Can it verify Firebase Console settings?
faq.answer: Only when the user provides those settings. Repository files alone do not expose every console configuration.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is intended for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that can inspect a Firebase repository.

faq.question: Is it a penetration testing tool?
faq.answer: No. It is a structured code and configuration review aid for Firebase projects.
